blocks.content: Wednesday. Jonathan found me at my locker at the end of the day.
He leaned against the locker next to mine. Arms crossed. He had that look he gets when he's going to say something that costs him something.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I been thinking about what you said.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I don't fully agree with it.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know. But I — I hear you. I hear what you were saying. And I been doing that. Since the beginning. Trying to steer you. And I told myself it was because I believed in you, which is true, but I also —
He stopped. He doesn't stop often. The stopping was its own thing.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I don't love watching you be slow. I know that's not a nice thing to say. But it's true. It makes me anxious. You move so careful and I keep wanting to just — push.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's a me thing. Not a you thing. I get that now.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Jonathan. You're one of the best people I know. And I don't need you to be different. I just need you to let me be me.
He looked at me. Really looked. Then he uncrossed his arms and stood up straight.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: No cap I've been sick about it since Thursday. It is not easy. But yeah. Deal.
Something settled. Not everything — you can't fix every fault line in one conversation. But the surface was solid again. That was enough for now.
